action.reasoning: Observation: Still OOM (group_norm in wav2vec2 encoder) on bs=64/256 after 163s run; no EER yet after 6 failed iters. History shows repeated collate/AMP/OOM issues now resolved except memory. Analysis: Root cause is large padded batches on variable-length audio (up to 15s) causing activation spikes in conv/feature layers during full fine-tune+eval; bf16+expandable_segments insufficient without length cap. Decision: Add max_length=160000+truncation=True to both collates (caps mem, most utts <5s), lower batches (train=16/dev=64/predict=128), fix autocast import+context to torch.amp('cuda', bfloat16) in train+predict for compatibility, keep 5 epochs+best-EER-save+full fine-tune (lr=1e-5). This gets working baseline per 'few epochs + immediate submit_eval' rule while measuring time; edit existing files minimally.
